title: Trading Mistake Schema
description: Schema for documenting trading mistakes for learning and improvement

id.description: Unique identifier for the mistake entry

description.example: Entered a 5:1 leverage long position on EURUSD during NFP release without checking the economic calendar. Market moved 200 pips against me in 45 seconds.

context.description: Context details about when and where the mistake occurred

what_went_wrong.example: Failed to check the economic calendar before trading. Did not adjust position size for elevated volatility. Emotions drove the decision to over-leverage after two winning trades.

correct_action.description: What should have been done instead
correct_action.example: Check economic calendar before entry. Reduce leverage to 1:2 during major economic events. Wait 30 minutes after NFP release before trading. Use a 1% risk per trade rule.

lesson_learned.description: Key takeaway and principle to apply
lesson_learned.example: Volatility adjustments are non-negotiable. Economic events create asymmetric risk-reward. Small consistent profits beat large risky moves.

created_at.description: When this mistake entry was created

example.title: Entered long position without stop loss
example.description: Saw a strong bullish setup on GBPUSD 4h chart and entered without setting a stop loss, expecting it to just work out. Price reversed sharply.
example.what_went_wrong: Overconfidence in the setup. Assumed I would have time to add a stop loss. Got distracted and let the loss run to -500 pips before closing.
example.correct_action: Always set stop loss at entry point. Use 1% risk rule: if account is $10k, max loss per trade is $100. Use alerts instead of relying on memory.
example.lesson_learned: A trading plan without a stop loss is not a plan. Discipline trumps prediction. 'I'll do it later' is a dangerous habit.
